evidence: QueryExecutor interface has 7 methods and one production implementation (BlackLabQueryExecutor). However, it is an adapter isolating the external BlackLab library — a justified one-impl interface.

SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles delegates entirely to comparator.compareCollocateProfiles — a pure pass-through. The method exists to keep the public explorer API stable, but the delegation adds an indirection hop with no policy.
ExploreResponseBuilder.buildEdges is a single-purpose static utility that does real translation work — the abstraction pays its cost.
HttpApiUtils is a tightly scoped static utility for HTTP mechanics (CORS, JSON response, param parsing) — well-bounded, not a dumping ground.

GrammarConfig.toJson() embeds JSON serialization logic inside the data object. The method uses fastjson2 JSONObject construction directly in the model class, mixing persistence/presentation concerns with data.

RelationConfig.collocatePosGroup() performs inline string scanning across the pattern field rather than operating on a structured intermediate representation. This is abstraction bypass: callers depend on a parsed POS group but the Config record exposes raw pattern scanning each call.

ExploreOptions bundles 4 parameters (topCollocates, nounsPerSeed, minLogDice, minShared). nounsPerSeed is documented as inapplicable to multi-seed paths — the single bag is slightly overfitted to single-seed, with dead weight for multi-seed callers.

The SemanticFieldExplorer null-GrammarConfig constructor (for testing) means CollocateProfileComparator also accepts null GrammarConfig, creating two parallel code paths (config-derived vs. hardcoded fallback). The fallback abstraction is never exercised in production but still exists as maintained code.

strengths: QueryExecutor interface is a textbook external-adapter abstraction — it isolates a complex third-party library (BlackLab) behind a clean, testable seam.
ExploreResponseBuilder is a focused translator with genuine logic (model-to-graph conversion), not a pass-through.
CqlUtils and LogDiceCalculator are small, coherent utility classes with no mixed concerns.
HttpApiUtils is a well-bounded static helper covering exactly the HTTP mechanics needed without becoming a catch-all.
abstraction_fitness.issue_character: Issues are localised: one pure-delegation pass-through (compareCollocateProfiles), one concern-mixing in a data object (GrammarConfig.toJson), and one inline computation that should be a derived property (collocatePosGroup pattern scanning).
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The codebase earns credit for the disciplined QueryExecutor adapter and the focused utility classes. Two abstractions don't fully pay their cost: compareCollocateProfiles in SemanticFieldExplorer adds an indirection hop that delivers no policy, and GrammarConfig.toJson mixes serialization into a data object. RelationConfig.collocatePosGroup re-scans the raw pattern string on every call rather than parsing to a richer intermediate, which is a leaky abstraction (callers ask for the parsed group but the record has no parsed state). These are real but fixable issues, keeping the score in the functional-but-uneven range rather than the solid band.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles is a pure delegation with no added policy
evidence: public ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(Set<String> seedNouns, double minLogDice, int maxPerNoun) throws IOException { return comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(seedNouns, minLogDice, maxPerNoun); }
The method signature, parameter names, and return type are identical to CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles. No validation, caching, logging, or transformation is added.
issues.suggestion: Either expose CollocateProfileComparator directly to callers (ExplorationHandlers), or add a genuine responsibility to SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles (e.g., input validation, logging at the explorer level, or post-processing). A zero-addition delegation wrapper bloats the call graph without benefit.

issues.summary: GrammarConfig.toJson() embeds fastjson2 serialization logic in a data object
evidence: GrammarConfig.toJson() directly constructs a JSONObject and JSONArray, coupling the model class to fastjson2.
RelationConfig.toJson() is similarly embedded in the record.
The serialization concern is only needed by SketchHandlers for the /api/relations endpoint; the config objects are used in many other places without needing JSON output.
issues.suggestion: Extract serialization to a GrammarConfigSerializer utility in the api or config package that accepts a GrammarConfig and returns JSONObject. This removes the fastjson2 import from config objects and allows changing the serialization format without touching the domain model.

issues.summary: RelationConfig.collocatePosGroup() re-scans the raw pattern string on every call with no parsed cache
evidence: collocatePosGroup() calls extractLabelContent() and posGroupFromConstraint() which scan the pattern string with string operations each invocation.
RelationConfig is a record (immutable), so the result of collocatePosGroup() is deterministic and could be computed once at construction time.
SemanticFieldExplorer.deriveNounCqlConstraint() calls collocatePosGroup() in a stream filter across all relations — repeated scanning for each element.
issues.suggestion: Add collocatePosGroup as a computed field derived in a static factory or add it as a constructor parameter in RelationConfig so it is parsed once. Alternatively, cache it lazily inside a wrapper object. This makes the derived nature of PosGroup explicit in the type rather than hiding it in a method body.

root_causes: The compareCollocateProfiles delegation was added when CollocateProfileComparator was extracted but the public API of SemanticFieldExplorer was preserved for callers; the delegation wrapper was not cleaned up.
toJson() methods were added early in development when config objects were also the serialization source; the responsibility split was deferred.
